check whether the matrix format is valid
        Parameters
        ----------
        full_check : bool, optional
            If `True`, rigorous check, O(N) operations. Otherwise
            basic check, O(1) operations (default True).

_cs_matrix.sumc
Reduce nonzeros with a ufunc over the minor axis when non-empty
        Can be applied to a function of self.data by supplying data parameter.
        Warning: this does not call sum_duplicates()
        Returns
        -------
        major_index : array of ints
            Major indices where nonzero
        value : array of self.dtype
            Reduce result for nonzeros in each major_index

_prepare_indices]
_cs_matrix._prepare_indicesc
Sets value at each (i, j) to x
        Here (i,j) index major and minor respectively, and must not contain
        duplicate entries.
        Fr
NzZChanging the sparsity structure of a {}_matrix is expensive. lil_matrix is more efficient.r/
_insert_many)
	n_samples
offsetsr
maskrU
_cs_matrix._set_manyc
Sets value at each (i, j) to zero, preserving sparsity structure.
        Here (i,j) index major and minor respectively.
        r(
_cs_matrix._zero_manyc
Inserts new nonzero at each (i, j) with value x
        Here (i,j) index major and minor respectively.
        i, j and x must be non-empty, 1d arrays.
        Inserts each major group (e.g. all entries per row) at a time.
        Maintains has_sorted_indices property.
        Modifies i, j, x in place.
        Z	mergesort)
clip)
moderW

_cs_matrix.toarrayc
zVRemove zero entries from the matrix
        This is an *in place* operation.
        N)
csr_eliminate_zerosrC
_cs_matrix.eliminate_zerosc
Determine whether the matrix has sorted indices and no duplicates
        Returns
            - True: if the above applies
            - False: otherwise
        has_canonical_format implies has_sorted_indices, so if the latter flag
        is False, so will the former be; if the former is found True, the
        latter flag is also set.
        
_has_sorted_indicesTF

_cs_matrix.resizec
Returns a matrix with the same sparsity structure as self,
        but with different data.  By default the structure arrays
        (i.e. .indptr and .indices) are copied.
        r-
